2606.28024.title: Lifted Causal Inference
2606.28024.paper_summary: Lifted inference exploits indistinguishabilities in probabilistic graphical models by using a representative for indistinguishable objects, thereby speeding up query answering while maintaining exact answers. In this article, we show how lifting can be applied to efficiently compute causal effects in relational domains. More specifically, we introduce parametric causal factor graphs (PCFGs) to incorporate causal knowledge in lifted models and give a formal semantics of interventions therein. We further present the Lifted Causal Inference (LCI) algorithm to compute causal effects on a lifted level, thereby drastically speeding up causal inference compared to propositional inference, e.g., in causal Bayesian networks. In addition, we present partially directed parametric causal factor graphs (PD-PCFGs) as a generalisation of PCFGs to handle partial causal knowledge and extend LCI to perform lifted causal inference in a PD-PCFG, thereby extending the applicability of lifted causal inference to a broader range of models requiring less prior knowledge about causal relationships.
2606.28024.paper_summary_zh: 提升推理利用概率圖模型中的不可區分性，通過使用不可區分物體的代表來加速查詢回答，同時保持精確的答案。<lb/>在本文中，我們展示了如何將提升應用於有效計算關聯領域中的因果效應。<lb/>更具體地說，我們引入了參數因果因子圖（PCFGs），以在提升模型中納入因果知識並給出干預的正式語義。<lb/>我們進一步提出了提升因果推理（LCI）算法，以在提升層面計算因果效應，從而相比於命題推理（例如，在因果貝葉斯網絡中）大幅加速因果推理。<lb/>此外，我們提出了部分定向參數因果因子圖（PD-PCFGs），作為PCFGs的推廣，以處理部分因果知識，並擴展LCI以在PD-PCFG中執行提升因果推理，從而將提升因果推理的適用性擴展到需要較少因果關係先驗知識的更廣泛模型。
2606.28024.author: Malte Luttermann et.al.
2606.28024.authors: Malte Luttermann, Tanya Braun, Ralf Möller, Marcel Gehrke
